acceptance_criteria.summary: Source Profile supports capabilities; account roles can overlap and have effective dates; unknown roles enter review.
All 6879 published records have complete raw-to-ledger lineage and no missing lineage.
Repeated import publishes zero additional ledger events with zero idempotency-key collisions.
Required event types have explicit impact flags; unresolved transfer/refund semantics fail closed and are not represented as confirmed chains.
Per-metric duplicate count is zero and all five declared pages share one snapshot read_model_hash.
All 1936 non-published records are partitioned into explicit review reasons; silent-drop count is zero.

stop_conditions.summary: Source records, normalized transactions, interconnection groups, economic events and ledger events are distinct and traceable.
No economic event is counted more than once per metric.
Account and event roles are policy-driven; source/provider names do not infer business categories.
Validation used an immutable Git-object snapshot and did not modify real data.

pass_gate_interpretation: Duplicate import and investment reconciliation pass. Transfer and refund candidates without required structured evidence pass only through fail-closed review routing; they are not confirmed chains.
human_traceability_scope: Any published metric event can be traced through the six-layer lineage identifiers without exposing private values in public evidence.
